title: Lockheed Martin F-35 Lightning II first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Lockheed Martin Aeronautics
fact: The Lockheed Martin F-35 Lightning II is an American family of single-seat, single-engine, supersonic stealth strike fighters.

title: Rockwell B-1 Lancer first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The Rockwell B-1 Lancer is a supersonic variable-sweep wing, heavy bomber used by the United States Air Force.

title: Northrop B-2 Spirit first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Northrop
fact: The Northrop B-2 Spirit is an American nuclear-capable subsonic stealth strategic bomber, often referred to as a "stealth bomber".

title: Boeing B-52 Stratofortress first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The Boeing B-52 Stratofortress is an American nuclear-capable subsonic jet-powered strategic bomber.

title: Northrop Grumman B-21 Raider first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Northrop Grumman Corporation
fact: The Northrop Grumman B-21 Raider is an American nuclear-capable subsonic stealth strategic bomber in development for the United States Air Force (USAF) by Northrop Grumman.

title: McDonnell Douglas F-15 Eagle first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by McDonnell Douglas
fact: The McDonnell Douglas F-15 Eagle is an American twin-engine, all-weather fighter aircraft designed by McDonnell Douglas.

title: Boeing E-3 Sentry first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Westinghouse Electric Corporation
fact: The Boeing E-3 Sentry is an American airborne early warning and control (AEW&C) aircraft developed by Boeing.

title: Lockheed Martin F-22 Raptor first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The Lockheed Martin–Boeing F-22 Raptor is an American twin-engine, jet-powered, all-weather, supersonic stealth fighter aircraft.

title: Fairchild Republic A-10 Thunderbolt II first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Fairchild Republic
fact: The Fairchild Republic A-10 Thunderbolt II, also widely known by the nickname Warthog, is a single-seat, twin-turbofan, straight-wing, subsonic attack aircraft developed by Fairchild Republic for the United States Air Force (USAF).

title: McDonnell Douglas F-15E Strike Eagle first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The McDonnell Douglas F-15E Strike Eagle is an American all-weather multirole strike fighter derived from the McDonnell Douglas F-15 Eagle.
Jas 39 Gripen gör sin första flygning.
subtitle: Svenskt stridsflygplan
title: Jas Gripen premiärflyger
Grumman F-14 Tomcat first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Grumman
fact: The Grumman F-14 Tomcat is an American carrier-capable supersonic, twin-engine, tandem two-seat, twin-tail, all-weather-capable variable-sweep wing fighter aircraft.

title: General Dynamics F-16 Fighting Falcon first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Lockheed Martin
fact: The General Dynamics F-16 Fighting Falcon is an American single-engine supersonic multirole fourth-generation fighter aircraft developed by General Dynamics and produced by multiple companies, including General Dynamics until 1993 and Lockheed Martin until 2017.
Saab 35 Draken gör sin första flygning.
subtitle: Svenskt stridsflygplan
title: Saab Draken premiärflyger

fact: Saab 37 Viggen gör sin första flygning.
subtitle: Svenskt stridsflygplan
title: Saab Viggen premiärflyger
Sukhoi Su-24 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Sukhoi
fact: The Sukhoi Su-24 is a supersonic, all-weather tactical bomber developed in the Soviet Union.

title: Eurofighter Typhoon first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Eurofighter Jagdflugzeug GmbH
fact: The Eurofighter Typhoon is a European multinational twin-engine, supersonic, canard delta wing, multirole fighter.

title: McDonnell Douglas F-4 Phantom II first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by McDonnell Douglas
fact: The McDonnell Douglas F-4 Phantom II is an American tandem two-seat, twin-engine, all-weather, long-range supersonic jet interceptor and fighter-bomber that was developed by McDonnell Aircraft for the United States Navy.

title: Sukhoi Su-57 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Komsomolsk-on-Amur Aircraft Production Association
fact: The Sukhoi Su-57 is a twin-engine stealth multirole fighter aircraft developed by Sukhoi.

title: Northrop F-5 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Northrop
fact: The Northrop F-5 is a family of supersonic light fighter aircraft initially designed as a privately funded project in the late 1950s by Northrop Corporation.

title: McDonnell Douglas F/A-18 Hornet first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The McDonnell Douglas F/A-18 Hornet is an all-weather supersonic, twin-engined, carrier-capable, multirole combat aircraft, designed as both a fighter and ground attack aircraft.

title: Lockheed SR-71 Blackbird first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Skunk Works
fact: The Lockheed SR-71 "Blackbird" is a retired long-range, high-altitude, Mach 3+ strategic reconnaissance aircraft that was developed and manufactured by the American aerospace company Lockheed Corporation.

title: Dassault Rafale first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Dassault Aviation
fact: The Dassault Rafale is a French twin-engine, canard delta wing, multirole fighter aircraft designed and built by Dassault Aviation.

title: KC-46 Pegasus first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The Boeing KC-46 Pegasus is an American military aerial refueling and strategic military transport aircraft developed by Boeing from its 767 jet airliner.

title: Boeing C-17 Globemaster III first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The McDonnell Douglas/Boeing C-17 Globemaster III is a large military transport aircraft developed for the United States Air Force (USAF) during the 1980s and the early 1990s by McDonnell Douglas.

title: Boeing E-7 Wedgetail first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The Boeing E-7 Wedgetail, also marketed as the Boeing 737 AEW&C, is a twin-engine airborne early warning and control aircraft originally designed for the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF).

title: Mikoyan MiG-29 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Russian Aircraft Corporation MiG
fact: The Mikoyan MiG-29 is a twin-engine fighter aircraft designed in the Soviet Union.

title: Boeing F/A-18 Super Hornet first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The Boeing F/A-18E and F/A-18F Super Hornet are a series of American supersonic twin-engine, carrier-capable, multirole fighter aircraft derived from the McDonnell Douglas F/A-18 Hornet.

title: Airbus A380 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Airbus SAS
fact: The Airbus A380 is a large wide-body airliner, developed and produced by Airbus until 2021.

title: Lockheed U-2 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Lockheed Corporation
fact: The Lockheed U-2, nicknamed the "Dragon Lady", is an American single-engine, high–altitude reconnaissance aircraft operated by the United States Air Force (USAF) and the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) since the 1950s.

title: Emirates is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: Emirates is one of the two flag carriers of the United Arab Emirates.

title: Sikorsky UH-60 Black Hawk first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Sikorsky Aircraft Corporation
fact: The Sikorsky UH-60 Black Hawk is a four-blade, twin-engine, medium-lift military utility helicopter manufactured by Sikorsky Aircraft.

title: Hughes AH-64 Apache first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by McDonnell Douglas
fact: The Hughes/McDonnell Douglas/Boeing AH-64 Apache is an American twin-turboshaft attack helicopter with a tailwheel-type landing gear and a tandem cockpit for a crew of two.

title: Chengdu J-7 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Chengdu Aircraft Industry Group
fact: The Chengdu J-7 is a Chinese fighter aircraft.

title: Boeing E-6 Mercury first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The Boeing E-6 Mercury is an airborne command post and communications relay based on the Boeing 707-300.

title: United Airlines is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: United Airlines, Inc. is a major airline in the United States headquartered in Chicago, Illinois.

title: Boeing CH-47 Chinook first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The Boeing CH-47 Chinook is a tandem-rotor helicopter originally developed by American rotorcraft company Vertol and now manufactured by Boeing Defense, Space & Security.

title: Airbus A350 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Airbus SAS
fact: The Airbus A350 is a long-range, wide-body twin-engine airliner developed and produced by Airbus.

title: Delta Air Lines is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: Delta Air Lines, Inc. is a major airline in the United States headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, operating nine hubs, with Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport being its largest in terms of total passengers and number of departures.

title: Lockheed C-5 Galaxy first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Lockheed Martin
fact: The Lockheed C-5 Galaxy is a large military transport aircraft designed and built by Lockheed, and now maintained and upgraded by its successor, Lockheed Martin.

title: Air Canada is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: Air Canada is the flag carrier and the largest airline of Canada, by size and passengers carried.

title: Boeing P-8 Poseidon first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing Defense, Space & Security
fact: The Boeing P-8 Poseidon is an American maritime patrol and reconnaissance aircraft developed and produced by Boeing Defense, Space & Security.

title: Airbus A330 MRTT first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Airbus Defence and Space
fact: The Airbus A330 Multi Role Tanker Transport (MRTT) is a European aerial refueling and military transport aircraft based on the civilian Airbus A330.

title: Etihad Airways is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: Etihad Airways is one of the two flag carriers of the United Arab Emirates.

title: Ryanair is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: Ryanair is an Irish ultra low-cost airline multinational group headquartered in Swords, County Dublin, Ireland.

title: Sukhoi Su-35 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Komsomolsk-on-Amur Aircraft Production Association
fact: The Sukhoi Su-35 is the designation for two improved derivatives of the Su-27 air superiority fighter.

title: Panavia Tornado first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Panavia Aircraft GmbH
fact: The Panavia Tornado is a family of twin-engine, variable-sweep wing multi-role combat aircraft, jointly developed and manufactured by Italy, the United Kingdom and Germany.

title: Bell/Boeing V-22 Osprey first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The Bell Boeing V-22 Osprey is an American multi-use, tiltrotor military transport and cargo aircraft with both vertical takeoff and landing (VTOL) and short takeoff and landing (STOL) capabilities.

title: American Airlines is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: American Airlines, Inc. is a major airline in the United States headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas, within the Dallas–Fort Worth metroplex, and is the largest airline in the world in terms of passengers carried and daily flights.

title: Sukhoi Su-17 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Sukhoi
fact: The Sukhoi Su-17 is a variable-sweep wing fighter-bomber developed for the Soviet military.

title: Sukhoi Su-27 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Sukhoi
fact: The Sukhoi Su-27 is a Soviet twin-engine supersonic supermaneuverable fighter aircraft designed by Sukhoi.

title: Boeing B-29 Superfortress first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The Boeing B-29 Superfortress is a retired American four-engined propeller-driven heavy bomber, designed by Boeing and flown primarily by the United States during World War II and the Korean War.

title: Lufthansa is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: Deutsche Lufthansa AG, trading as the Lufthansa Group, is a German aviation holding company.

title: Airbus A400M Atlas first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Airbus Military
fact: The Airbus A400M Atlas is a European four-engine turboprop military transport aircraft.

title: Supermarine Spitfire first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Supermarine
fact: The Supermarine Spitfire is a British single-seat fighter aircraft that was used by the Royal Air Force and other Allied countries before, during, and after World War II.

title: Cathay Pacific is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: Cathay Pacific Airways Limited, or simply Cathay Pacific, is the flag carrier of Hong Kong, with its head office and main hub located at Hong Kong International Airport.

title: IndiGo is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: IndiGo is an Indian low-cost hybrid airline headquartered in Gurgaon, Haryana.

title: British Airways is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: British Airways plc (BA) is the flag carrier of the United Kingdom.

title: Korea Aerospace Industries KF-21 Boramae first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Korea Aerospace Industries
fact: The KAI KF-21 Boramae is a South Korean twin-engine fighter aircraft.

title: Heron-1 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Israel Aerospace Industries Ltd
fact: The IAI Heron (Machatz-1) is a medium-altitude long-endurance unmanned aerial vehicle developed by the Malat (UAV) division of Israel Aerospace Industries.

title: Mil Mi-24 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Rostvertol
fact: The Mil Mi-24 is a large helicopter gunship, attack helicopter and low-capacity troop transport with room for eight passengers.

title: Chengdu J-10 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Chengdu Aircraft Industry Group
fact: The Chengdu J-10, also known as Menglong is a Chinese medium-weight, single-engine, multirole combat aircraft using a delta wing and canard design, with a maximum speed of Mach 1.8.

title: Airbus A330 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Airbus SAS
fact: The Airbus A330 is a wide-body airliner developed and produced by Airbus.

title: Qatar Airways is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: Qatar Airways Company Q.C.S.C., operating as Qatar Airways, is the flag carrier of Qatar.

title: Mikoyan-Gurevich MiG-21 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Tbilisi Aircraft Manufacturing
fact: The Mikoyan-Gurevich MiG-21 is a supersonic jet fighter and interceptor aircraft, designed by the Mikoyan-Gurevich Design Bureau in the Soviet Union.

title: Boeing B-17 Flying Fortress first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The Boeing B-17 Flying Fortress is an American four-engined heavy bomber aircraft that was developed in the mid-1930s for the United States Army Air Corps (USAAC).

title: Northrop T-38 Talon first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Northrop
fact: The Northrop T-38 Talon is a two-seat, twinjet supersonic jet trainer designed and produced by the American aircraft manufacturer Northrop Corporation.

title: Airbus A320 family first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Airbus SAS
fact: The Airbus A320 family is a series of narrow-body airliners developed and produced by Airbus, and is the best-selling airliner ever built.

title: C-130J Super Hercules first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Lockheed Martin
fact: The Lockheed Martin C-130J Super Hercules is a medium-size American four-engine turboprop military transport aircraft.

title: CAC/PAC JF-17 Thunder first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Chengdu Aircraft Industry Group
fact: The CAC/PAC JF-17 Thunder, also known as FC-1 Xiaolong, is a Sino-Pakistani single-engine lightweight supersonic multirole combat aircraft developed jointly by the Pakistan Aeronautical Complex (PAC) and the Chengdu Aircraft Corporation (CAC) of China.

title: North American P-51 Mustang first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by North American Aviation
fact: The North American Aviation P-51 Mustang is an American long-range, single-seat fighter and fighter-bomber used during World War II and the Korean War, among other conflicts.

title: Tejas first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited
fact: The HAL Tejas is an Indian single-engine, 4.5 generation, delta wing, multirole combat aircraft designed by the Aeronautical Development Agency and manufactured by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) for the Indian Air Force (IAF) and the Indian Navy.

title: Air India is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: Air India is the flag carrier of India, headquartered in Gurugram, Haryana.

title: E-4 Advanced Airborne Command Post first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Boeing
fact: The Boeing E-4 Advanced Airborne Command Post (AACP), the current "Nightwatch" aircraft, is a series of strategic command and control military aircraft operated by the United States Air Force (USAF).

title: E-Jet first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Embraer
fact: The Embraer E-Jet family is a series of four-abreast, narrow-body, short- to medium-range, twin-engined regional jet airliners designed and produced by Brazilian aerospace manufacturer Embraer.

title: Mil Mi-17 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Kazan Helicopters
fact: The Mil Mi-17 is a Soviet-designed Russian military helicopter family introduced in 1975 (Mi-8M), continuing in production as of 2024 at two factories in Russia, in Kazan and Ulan-Ude.

title: De Havilland Canada DHC-8 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by De Havilland Canada
fact: The De Havilland Canada DHC-8, commonly known as the Dash 8, is a series of turboprop-powered regional airliners, introduced by de Havilland Canada (DHC) in 1984.

title: Korea Aerospace Industries T-50 Golden Eagle first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Korea Aerospace Industries
fact: The KAI T-50 Golden Eagle (Korean: 골든이글) is a family of advanced, supersonic, South Korean jet trainers, light combat aircraft, light strike fighters and multirole light fighters developed by Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) with Lockheed Martin.

title: Pan Am is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: Pan American World Airways, originally founded as Pan American Airways and more commonly known as Pan Am, was an airline that was the principal and largest international air carrier and unofficial flag carrier of the United States for much of the 20th century.

title: Airbus A220 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Airbus Canada
fact: The Airbus A220 is a family of five-abreast narrow-body airliners by Airbus Canada Limited Partnership (ACLP).

title: Mikoyan MiG-31 first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Russian Aircraft Corporation MiG
fact: The Mikoyan MiG-31 is a supersonic interceptor aircraft developed for the Soviet Air Forces by the Mikoyan design bureau as a replacement for the MiG-25 "Foxbat", on which it is based and shares design elements.

title: Bell UH-1 Iroquois first flies
subtitle: Aircraft by Bell Textron
fact: The Bell UH-1 Iroquois is a utility military helicopter designed and produced by the American aerospace company Bell Helicopter.

title: Southwest Airlines is founded
subtitle: Airline
fact: Southwest Airlines is a major airline in the United States.
